name: Spanish A2 Grammar
description: Master CEFR A2 Spanish grammar — from past tenses (preterite and imperfect) and present perfect to reflexive verbs, object pronouns, comparatives, and the affirmative imperative. Fill in the blank to prove you know which form fits. Scoped from the Instituto Cervantes PCIC A2 curriculum.

facts.quizQuestion:
Lo hago {___} ti — quiero ayudarte.
(I do it for you — I want to help you. [on your behalf])
facts.sentenceTranslation: I do it for you — I want to help you.
facts.grammarPointLabel: por — on behalf of / for someone's sake
facts.explanation: por — used when doing something on behalf of someone or for their sake. Contrast: 'para ti' = for you (recipient/gift), 'por ti' = for your sake/on your behalf.

facts.quizQuestion:
Gracias {___} todo.
(Thank you for everything.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Thank you for everything.
facts.explanation: por — 'gracias por' = thank you for. In 'gracias por todo', 'por' expresses the reason/cause for gratitude. Always 'gracias por', never 'gracias para'.

facts.quizQuestion:
Pagué diez euros {___} el libro.
(I paid ten euros for the book.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I paid ten euros for the book.
facts.explanation: por — used for exchange or price. 'Pagué X por Y' = I paid X for Y. Always 'por' with pagué, vendí, cambié, etc. (never 'para').

facts.quizQuestion:
Normalmente estudio {___} la tarde.
(I usually study in the afternoon.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I usually study in the afternoon.
facts.grammarPointLabel: por — time of day (por la mañana/tarde/noche)
facts.explanation: por — used with parts of the day: por la mañana (in the morning), por la tarde (in the afternoon/evening), por la noche (at night). Set expressions, always with 'por'.

facts.quizQuestion:
Estaba enfermo, {___} no fui al trabajo.
(I was sick, that's why I didn't go to work.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I was sick, that's why I didn't go to work.
facts.explanation: por eso — means 'because of that / that's why'. Common connector in Spanish. 'Por' expresses cause here.

facts.quizQuestion:
Estudio {___} aprender español.
(I study in order to learn Spanish.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I study in order to learn Spanish.
facts.explanation: para — used with infinitive to express purpose/goal. 'Estudio para aprender' = I study (in order) to learn. This is the most common use of 'para'.

facts.quizQuestion:
Este regalo es {___} ti.
(This gift is for you.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: This gift is for you.
facts.explanation: para — used for the recipient of something. 'Es para ti' = it's for you (you will receive it). Contrast: 'por ti' = for your sake (I acted on your behalf).

facts.quizQuestion:
Mañana salgo {___} Madrid.
(Tomorrow I leave for Madrid.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Tomorrow I leave for Madrid.
facts.explanation: para — used for destination/direction when expressing travel toward a place. 'Salir para Madrid' = to leave for Madrid (destination). Different from 'ir a Madrid'.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} mí, es muy difícil.
(For me (in my opinion), it is very difficult.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: For me (in my opinion), it is very difficult.
facts.explanation: para — used for expressing one's perspective or opinion. 'Para mí' = in my opinion / from my perspective. Commonly used in conversation.

facts.quizQuestion:
Este libro es {___} interesante.
(This book is very interesting.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: This book is very interesting.
facts.explanation: muy — used before adjectives and adverbs: muy interesante, muy bien. Never changes form. Contrast: mucho (used with nouns, changes gender/number).

facts.quizQuestion:
Habla español {___} bien.
(He speaks Spanish very well.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He speaks Spanish very well.
facts.explanation: muy — used before adverbs too: muy bien (very well), muy rápido (very fast). Invariable.

facts.quizQuestion:
Tengo {___} trabajo esta semana.
(I have a lot of work this week.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I have a lot of work this week.
facts.explanation: mucho — used before masculine singular nouns: mucho trabajo, mucho tiempo. Agrees in gender/number with the noun.

facts.quizQuestion:
Hay {___} gente en el mercado hoy.
(There are a lot of people at the market today.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: There are a lot of people at the market today.
facts.explanation: mucha — feminine form of mucho. Used before feminine singular nouns: mucha gente, mucha agua, mucha suerte. Must agree in gender.

facts.quizQuestion:
Mi jefe trabaja {___}.
(My boss works a lot.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: My boss works a lot.
facts.explanation: mucho — when used as an adverb after a verb, mucho is invariable: trabaja mucho, come mucho, duerme mucho. Don't confuse with adjective use.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} mañana.
(I'm going to call you tomorrow.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'm going to call you tomorrow.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ir a + infinitivo (near future): voy a llamar
facts.explanation: voy a llamar — near future structure. ir (present) + a + infinitive. Expresses planned or imminent future actions. More common than the future tense in spoken Spanish.

facts.quizQuestion:
Mira esas nubes — {___}.
(Look at those clouds — it's going to rain.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Look at those clouds — it's going to rain.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ir a + infinitivo: va a llover (it's going to rain)
facts.explanation: va a llover — third person of ir a + infinitivo. 'Va a llover' = it's going to rain. Weather expressions use the 3rd person singular impersonal.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} fui al parque con mi familia.
(Yesterday I went to the park with my family.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Yesterday I went to the park with my family.
facts.explanation: ayer — yesterday. Always used with the preterite (completed action). One of the clearest triggers for preterite tense.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} trabajé mucho en el proyecto.
(Last week I worked a lot on the project.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Last week I worked a lot on the project.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Time expression with preterite: la semana pasada
facts.explanation: la semana pasada — last week. Time expressions with 'pasado/a' (past) signal the preterite: la semana pasada, el mes pasado, el año pasado.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} compré esta casa.
(Two years ago I bought this house.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Two years ago I bought this house.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Time expression: hace X tiempo (X ago)
facts.explanation: hace dos años — two years ago. Structure: hace + time period. Always with preterite for completed actions. 'hace' is invariable.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} viajé a Argentina.
(Last year I traveled to Argentina.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Last year I traveled to Argentina.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Time expression with preterite: el año pasado
facts.explanation: el año pasado — last year. Like 'la semana pasada', signals the preterite for a completed past event.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} he terminado los deberes.
(I haven't finished my homework yet.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I haven't finished my homework yet.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Present perfect time expression: todavía no (not yet)
facts.explanation: todavía no — 'not yet'. Used with present perfect to indicate something hasn't happened up to now. Also: aún no. Opposite: ya (already).

facts.quizQuestion:
¿Has comido {___} sushi?
(Have you ever eaten sushi?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Have you ever eaten sushi?
facts.grammarPointLabel: Present perfect time expression: alguna vez (ever)
facts.explanation: alguna vez — 'ever' (in questions). Used with present perfect in questions: ¿Has + PP + alguna vez? Common structure for asking about life experiences.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} vivía en una ciudad pequeña.
(Before, I used to live in a small town.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Before, I used to live in a small town.
facts.explanation: antes — 'before / previously'. One of the strongest imperfect triggers. Indicates a habitual or ongoing past state that has changed.

facts.quizQuestion:
Cuando {___} pequeño, jugaba mucho al fútbol.
(When I was young, I used to play football a lot.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: When I was young, I used to play football a lot.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Imperfect: cuando era pequeño (when I was young)
facts.explanation: era — imperfect of ser. 'Cuando era pequeño/a' (when I was young) is a standard phrase for talking about childhood habits. Sets up imperfect for both verbs.

facts.quizQuestion:
El sábado {___} una excursión a la montaña.
(On Saturday we went on a trip to the mountains.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: On Saturday we went on a trip to the mountains.
facts.explanation: hicimos — nosotros form of hacer in the preterite. Irregular: hicim- + -os.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} la verdad sobre lo que pasó.
(We told the truth about what happened.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We told the truth about what happened.
facts.explanation: dijimos — nosotros form of decir in the preterite. Irregular: dijim- + -os.

facts.quizQuestion:
Anoche {___} el partido de fútbol.
(Last night we watched the football match.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Last night we watched the football match.
facts.explanation: vimos — nosotros form of ver in the preterite. Irregular: vim- + -os.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} juntos en el mismo autobús.
(We came together on the same bus.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We came together on the same bus.
facts.explanation: vinimos — nosotros form of venir in the preterite. Irregular: vinim- + -os.

facts.quizQuestion:
El verano pasado {___} en la playa dos semanas.
(Last summer we were at the beach for two weeks.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Last summer we were at the beach for two weeks.
facts.explanation: estuvimos — nosotros form of estar in the preterite. Irregular: estuvim- + -os.

facts.quizQuestion:
María no puede venir ahora, {___} por teléfono.
(María can't come now, she is talking on the phone.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: María can't come now, she is talking on the phone.
facts.explanation: está hablando — estar (present, él/ella) + gerund. The progressive expresses an action happening right now, at this exact moment.

facts.quizQuestion:
Los fines de semana {___} tarde.
(On weekends we get up late.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: On weekends we get up late.
facts.explanation: nos levantamos — nosotros form of levantarse. Reflexive pronoun 'nos' (nosotros) + levantamos.

facts.quizQuestion:
En esa tienda {___} coches de segunda mano.
(In that store used cars are sold.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: In that store used cars are sold.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Impersonal se: se venden (plural — cars for sale)
facts.explanation: se venden — impersonal se, plural. The verb agrees with the noun (plural noun 'coches' → plural verb 'venden'). Compare: se vende piso (singular).

facts.quizQuestion:
Él habla {___} yo.
(He speaks faster than me.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He speaks faster than me.
facts.explanation: más rápido que — comparative used with adverbs too: más rápido que (faster than), más despacio que (more slowly than). Same structure as with adjectives.

facts.quizQuestion:
¿{___} juntos esta tarde?
(Are we going to eat together this afternoon?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Are we going to eat together this afternoon?
facts.grammarPointLabel: ir a + infinitivo (nosotros): vamos a comer
facts.explanation: vamos a comer — nosotros near future. 'Vamos a + infinitive'. Note: 'vamos a' can also mean 'let's': ¡Vamos a comer! (Let's eat!).

facts.quizQuestion:
¿{___} alguna vez en el extranjero?
(Have you ever lived abroad?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Have you ever lived abroad?
facts.explanation: has vivido — tú form of present perfect. 'Has' = you have. Regular -ir past participle: viv- + -ido.

facts.quizQuestion:
De joven, {___} muy activo.
(As a young man, he was very active.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: As a young man, he was very active.
facts.explanation: era — él/ella form of ser in the imperfect. Note: same form as yo: yo era, él era (context clarifies). Different from estar imperfect: estaba.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ayer yo {___} con mi madre por teléfono.
(Yesterday I spoke with my mother on the phone.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Yesterday I spoke with my mother on the phone.
facts.explanation: hablé — yo form of hablar in the preterite. Regular -ar: habl- + -é. Used for a completed action at a specific past time.

facts.quizQuestion:
¿{___} con Tom ayer?
(Did you talk to Tom yesterday?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Did you talk to Tom yesterday?
facts.explanation: hablaste — tú form of hablar in the preterite. Regular -ar: habl- + -aste.

facts.quizQuestion:
Mi profesor {___} muy rápido en la clase.
(My teacher spoke very fast in class.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: My teacher spoke very fast in class.
facts.explanation: habló — él/ella form of hablar in the preterite. Regular -ar: habl- + -ó. Note the written accent.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ayer {___} del problema en la reunión.
(Yesterday we talked about the problem in the meeting.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Yesterday we talked about the problem in the meeting.
facts.explanation: hablamos — nosotros form of hablar in the preterite. Same as present tense. Context clarifies which tense.

facts.quizQuestion:
Los niños {___} en voz baja.
(The children spoke in a low voice.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The children spoke in a low voice.
facts.explanation: hablaron — ellos/ellas/ustedes form of hablar in the preterite. Regular -ar: habl- + -aron.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ayer {___} un libro en la librería.
(Yesterday I bought a book at the bookstore.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Yesterday I bought a book at the bookstore.
facts.explanation: compré — yo form of comprar in the preterite. Regular -ar: compr- + -é.

facts.quizQuestion:
Tom {___} una casa nueva el año pasado.
(Tom bought a new house last year.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Tom bought a new house last year.
facts.explanation: compró — él/ella form of comprar in the preterite. Regular -ar: compr- + -ó.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} tarde al trabajo ayer.
(I arrived late to work yesterday.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I arrived late to work yesterday.
facts.explanation: llegué — yo form of llegar in the preterite. Spelling change: g→gu before -é to keep the /g/ sound.

facts.quizQuestion:
Tom {___} tarde a la reunión.
(Tom arrived late to the meeting.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Tom arrived late to the meeting.
facts.explanation: llegó — él/ella form of llegar in the preterite. Regular -ar: lleg- + -ó.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ayer {___} mucho y estoy muy cansado.
(Yesterday I worked a lot and I am very tired.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Yesterday I worked a lot and I am very tired.
facts.explanation: trabajé — yo form of trabajar in the preterite. Regular -ar: trabaj- + -é.

facts.quizQuestion:
Te {___} ayer pero no contestaste.
(I called you yesterday but you didn't answer.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I called you yesterday but you didn't answer.
facts.explanation: llamé — yo form of llamar in the preterite. Regular -ar: llam- + -é.

facts.quizQuestion:
¿Qué {___} aquí anoche?
(What happened here last night?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: What happened here last night?
facts.explanation: pasó — él/ella form of pasar in the preterite. ¿Qué pasó? (What happened?) is one of the most common preterite phrases.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} a estudiar español hace un año.
(I started studying Spanish one year ago.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I started studying Spanish one year ago.
facts.explanation: empecé — yo form of empezar in the preterite. Spelling change: z→c before -é to keep the /θ/ sound.

facts.quizQuestion:
Anoche {___} demasiado en la cena.
(Last night I ate too much at dinner.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Last night I ate too much at dinner.
facts.explanation: comí — yo form of comer in the preterite. Regular -er: com- + -í.

facts.quizQuestion:
¿{___} algo esta mañana?
(Did you eat anything this morning?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Did you eat anything this morning?
facts.explanation: comiste — tú form of comer in the preterite. Regular -er: com- + -iste.

facts.quizQuestion:
Los estudiantes {___} en el comedor.
(The students ate in the cafeteria.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The students ate in the cafeteria.
facts.explanation: comieron — ellos form of comer in the preterite. Regular -er: com- + -ieron.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} agua durante todo el día.
(I drank water throughout the whole day.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I drank water throughout the whole day.
facts.explanation: bebí — yo form of beber in the preterite. Regular -er: beb- + -í.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} ese libro en dos días.
(I read that book in two days.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I read that book in two days.
facts.explanation: leí — yo form of leer in the preterite. Regular -er: le- + -í. Note: leer has a y in the 3rd person (leyó, leyeron).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} en Madrid durante tres años.
(I lived in Madrid for three years.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I lived in Madrid for three years.
facts.explanation: viví — yo form of vivir in the preterite. Regular -ir: viv- + -í.

facts.quizQuestion:
¿{___} en España mucho tiempo?
(Did you live in Spain for a long time?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Did you live in Spain for a long time?
facts.explanation: viviste — tú form of vivir in the preterite. Regular -ir: viv- + -iste.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} tarde anoche.
(I went out late last night.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I went out late last night.
facts.explanation: salí — yo form of salir in the preterite. Regular -ir: sal- + -í.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} una carta a mis padres ayer.
(I wrote a letter to my parents yesterday.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I wrote a letter to my parents yesterday.
facts.explanation: escribí — yo form of escribir in the preterite. Regular -ir: escrib- + -í.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ayer {___} al médico.
(Yesterday I went to the doctor.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Yesterday I went to the doctor.
facts.explanation: fui — yo form of ir (and also ser) in the preterite. Ser and ir are identical in the preterite — context determines meaning.

facts.quizQuestion:
¿{___} a la fiesta el sábado?
(Did you go to the party on Saturday?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Did you go to the party on Saturday?
facts.explanation: fuiste — tú form of ir in the preterite. Irregular: the fui- stem is shared with ser.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ayer {___} un día muy bueno.
(Yesterday was a very good day.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Yesterday was a very good day.
facts.explanation: fue — él/ella form of ser (and ir) in the preterite. Here it means 'was' (ser). Context: 'fue un día' = 'it was a day'.

facts.quizQuestion:
El domingo {___} al parque.
(On Sunday we went to the park.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: On Sunday we went to the park.
facts.explanation: fuimos — nosotros form of ir in the preterite. Irregular: fuimos (ir/ser).

facts.quizQuestion:
Ellos {___} a la playa el verano pasado.
(They went to the beach last summer.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They went to the beach last summer.
facts.explanation: fueron — ellos/ellas/ustedes form of ir (and ser) in the preterite.

facts.quizQuestion:
El fin de semana {___} en casa todo el día.
(On the weekend I was at home all day.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: On the weekend I was at home all day.
facts.explanation: estuve — yo form of estar in the preterite. Irregular: estuv- stem + -e ending.

facts.quizQuestion:
Mi hermano {___} enfermo la semana pasada.
(My brother was sick last week.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: My brother was sick last week.
facts.explanation: estuvo — él/ella form of estar in the preterite. Irregular: estuv- + -o.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ayer {___} un problema con el coche.
(Yesterday I had a problem with the car.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Yesterday I had a problem with the car.
facts.explanation: tuve — yo form of tener in the preterite. Irregular: tuv- stem + -e ending.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} mucha suerte en ese examen.
(You were very lucky on that exam.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You were very lucky on that exam.
facts.explanation: tuviste — tú form of tener in the preterite. Irregular: tuv- + -iste.

facts.quizQuestion:
Anoche {___} los deberes.
(Last night I did my homework.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Last night I did my homework.
facts.explanation: hice — yo form of hacer in the preterite. Irregular: hic- stem + -e. Note: spelling change c→z in 3rd person (hizo).

facts.quizQuestion:
¿Qué {___} ayer por la tarde?
(What did you do yesterday afternoon?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: What did you do yesterday afternoon?
facts.explanation: hiciste — tú form of hacer in the preterite. Irregular: hicist- + -e.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ayer {___} mucho frío en la ciudad.
(Yesterday it was very cold in the city.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Yesterday it was very cold in the city.
facts.explanation: hizo — él/ella form of hacer in the preterite. Note c→z spelling change (hice → hizo) to maintain the same /θ/ sound.

facts.quizQuestion:
Lo {___} claramente: no quiero ir.
(I said it clearly: I don't want to go.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I said it clearly: I don't want to go.
facts.explanation: dije — yo form of decir in the preterite. Irregular: dij- stem + -e.

facts.quizQuestion:
Él {___} que sí.
(He said yes.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He said yes.
facts.explanation: dijo — él/ella form of decir in the preterite. Irregular: dij- + -o.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ayer {___} una película muy buena.
(Yesterday I watched a very good film.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Yesterday I watched a very good film.
facts.explanation: vi — yo form of ver in the preterite. Irregular and short: no accent needed. ver → vi (yo), viste (tú), vio (él).

facts.quizQuestion:
¿{___} el partido de fútbol anoche?
(Did you see the football match last night?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Did you see the football match last night?
facts.explanation: viste — tú form of ver in the preterite. Irregular: v- + -iste.

facts.quizQuestion:
Le {___} el libro a María.
(I gave the book to María.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I gave the book to María.
facts.explanation: di — yo form of dar in the preterite. Irregular and short: like ver, dar uses the -er/-ir endings (di, diste, dio...) despite being -ar.

facts.quizQuestion:
Él me {___} un regalo de cumpleaños.
(He gave me a birthday present.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He gave me a birthday present.
facts.explanation: dio — él/ella form of dar in the preterite. Irregular -ar verb using -er/-ir endings.

facts.quizQuestion:
Anoche no {___} dormir por el ruido.
(Last night I couldn't sleep because of the noise.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Last night I couldn't sleep because of the noise.
facts.explanation: pude — yo form of poder in the preterite. Irregular: pud- stem + -e.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} la mesa para la cena.
(I set the table for dinner.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I set the table for dinner.
facts.explanation: puse — yo form of poner in the preterite. Irregular: pus- stem + -e.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} a verte en cuanto pude.
(I came to see you as soon as I could.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I came to see you as soon as I could.
facts.explanation: vine — yo form of venir in the preterite. Irregular: vin- stem + -e.

facts.quizQuestion:
¿Por qué {___} tan tarde?
(Why did you come so late?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Why did you come so late?
facts.explanation: viniste — tú form of venir in the preterite. Irregular: vin- + -iste.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} llamarte pero no tenía batería.
(I wanted to call you but I had no battery.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I wanted to call you but I had no battery.
facts.explanation: quise — yo form of querer in the preterite. Irregular: quis- stem + -e. In preterite, querer often means 'tried to' or 'wanted to (and did/didn't)'.

facts.quizQuestion:
Lo {___} ayer por la mañana.
(I found out (I knew) yesterday morning.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I found out yesterday morning.
facts.explanation: supe — yo form of saber in the preterite. Irregular: sup- stem + -e. In preterite, saber means 'found out' (learned for the first time).

facts.quizQuestion:
De niño, {___} mucho con mis amigos.
(As a child, I talked a lot with my friends.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: As a child, I talked a lot with my friends.
facts.explanation: hablaba — yo/él/ella form of hablar in the imperfect. Regular -ar: habl- + -aba. Used for habitual/repeated past actions.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} muy rápido cuando eras pequeño.
(You used to speak very fast when you were young.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You used to speak very fast when you were young.
facts.explanation: hablabas — tú form of hablar in the imperfect. Regular -ar: habl- + -abas.

facts.quizQuestion:
Antes {___} todos los días por teléfono.
(Before, we used to talk every day by phone.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Before, we used to talk every day by phone.
facts.explanation: hablábamos — nosotros form of hablar in the imperfect. Regular -ar: habl- + -ábamos. Note the written accent.

facts.quizQuestion:
Los estudiantes {___} cuando entré a la clase.
(The students were talking when I entered the classroom.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The students were talking when I entered the classroom.
facts.explanation: hablaban — ellos form of hablar in the imperfect. Regular -ar: habl- + -aban. Describes an action that was ongoing when something else happened.

facts.quizQuestion:
Antes {___} en una empresa grande.
(Before, I used to work at a large company.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Before, I used to work at a large company.
facts.explanation: trabajaba — yo/él form of trabajar in the imperfect. Regular -ar: trabaj- + -aba.

facts.quizQuestion:
Cuando {___} en París, iba mucho al museo.
(When I was living in Paris, I went to the museum a lot.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: When I was living in Paris, I went to the museum a lot.
facts.explanation: vivía — yo/él form of vivir in the imperfect. Regular -ir: viv- + -ía.

facts.quizQuestion:
De niño {___} mucho chocolate.
(As a child I used to eat a lot of chocolate.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: As a child I used to eat a lot of chocolate.
facts.explanation: comía — yo/él form of comer in the imperfect. Regular -er: com- + -ía.

facts.quizQuestion:
Antes, todos {___} en familia.
(Before, everyone used to eat together as a family.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Before, everyone used to eat together as a family.
facts.explanation: comían — ellos form of comer in the imperfect. Regular -er: com- + -ían.

facts.quizQuestion:
Cuando {___} niño, quería ser astronauta.
(When I was a child, I wanted to be an astronaut.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: When I was a child, I wanted to be an astronaut.
facts.explanation: era — yo/él/ella form of ser in the imperfect. Ser is irregular in the imperfect: er- + -a (yo/él/ella).

facts.quizQuestion:
De niño {___} muy tímido.
(As a child you were very shy.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: As a child you were very shy.
facts.explanation: eras — tú form of ser in the imperfect. Irregular: er- + -as.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} muy buenos amigos en la escuela.
(We were very good friends in school.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We were very good friends in school.
facts.explanation: éramos — nosotros form of ser in the imperfect. Irregular: éram- + -os. Note the written accent on the first é.

facts.quizQuestion:
Mis abuelos {___} muy trabajadores.
(My grandparents were very hardworking.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: My grandparents were very hardworking.
facts.explanation: eran — ellos form of ser in the imperfect. Irregular: er- + -an.

facts.quizQuestion:
De niño {___} al parque todos los domingos.
(As a child I used to go to the park every Sunday.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: As a child I used to go to the park every Sunday.
facts.explanation: iba — yo/él form of ir in the imperfect. Ir is irregular: ib- + -a (yo/él/ella).

facts.quizQuestion:
¿Siempre {___} a la misma escuela?
(Did you always go to the same school?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Did you always go to the same school?
facts.explanation: ibas — tú form of ir in the imperfect. Irregular: ib- + -as.

facts.quizQuestion:
Antes {___} de vacaciones juntos cada verano.
(Before, we used to go on vacation together every summer.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Before, we used to go on vacation together every summer.
facts.explanation: íbamos — nosotros form of ir in the imperfect. Irregular: íbam- + -os. Note the accent.

facts.quizQuestion:
De pequeño {___} mucho la televisión.
(As a young child I used to watch TV a lot.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: As a young child I used to watch TV a lot.
facts.explanation: veía — yo/él form of ver in the imperfect. Ver is irregular: ve- + -ía (not the expected *vía).

facts.quizQuestion:
¿Cuándo {___} a tus amigos normalmente?
(When did you normally see your friends?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: When did you normally see your friends?
facts.explanation: veías — tú form of ver in the imperfect. Irregular: ve- + -ías.

facts.quizQuestion:
Mis padres {___} las noticias cada noche.
(My parents used to watch the news every night.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: My parents used to watch the news every night.
facts.explanation: veían — ellos form of ver in the imperfect. Irregular: ve- + -ían.

facts.quizQuestion:
Cuando llegué a casa, él {___}.
(When I arrived home, he was sleeping.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: When I arrived home, he was sleeping.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Preterite vs. Imperfect contrast: background action
facts.explanation: dormía — imperfect of dormir (to sleep). The preterite 'llegué' describes the completed action; the imperfect 'dormía' describes the ongoing background action at that moment.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} tarde cuando llegué.
(It was late when I arrived.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It was late when I arrived.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Preterite vs. Imperfect contrast: past setting
facts.explanation: era — imperfect of ser. 'Era tarde' sets the background context (imperfect); 'llegué' is the completed action that happened then (preterite).

facts.quizQuestion:
Hoy {___} con el director.
(Today I have spoken with the director.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Today I have spoken with the director.
facts.explanation: he hablado — present perfect of hablar. Formed with haber (present) + past participle. 'He' = I have (auxiliary). Used for recent or relevant past actions.

facts.quizQuestion:
¿{___} alguna vez con una persona famosa?
(Have you ever spoken with a famous person?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Have you ever spoken with a famous person?
facts.explanation: has hablado — tú form of present perfect. 'Has' = you have (auxiliary). 'Alguna vez' = ever.

facts.quizQuestion:
Hoy ya {___} dos veces.
(Today I have already eaten twice.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Today I have already eaten twice.
facts.explanation: he comido — present perfect of comer. Regular -er past participle: com- + -ido.

facts.quizQuestion:
Esta semana {___} mucho ejercicio.
(This week I have done a lot of exercise.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: This week I have done a lot of exercise.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Present perfect: hacer (yo) — he + hecho (irregular PP)
facts.explanation: he hecho — present perfect of hacer. Irregular past participle: hecho (NOT 'hacido'). One of the most common irregular past participles.

facts.quizQuestion:
¿{___} alguna vez esa película?
(Have you ever seen that film?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Have you ever seen that film?
facts.grammarPointLabel: Present perfect: ver — he visto (irregular PP)
facts.explanation: he visto — present perfect of ver. Irregular past participle: visto (NOT 'vido'). Very common in conversation.

facts.quizQuestion:
¿Qué has {___} hoy?
(What have you done today?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: What have you done today?
facts.explanation: hecho — irregular past participle of hacer. Common irregulars to memorize: hacer→hecho, decir→dicho, ver→visto, volver→vuelto, escribir→escrito, poner→puesto.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ya lo he {___} muchas veces.
(I have already said it many times.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I have already said it many times.
facts.explanation: dicho — irregular past participle of decir (to say/tell). The -cho ending is a pattern for hacer and decir.

facts.quizQuestion:
¿Ha {___} ya de su viaje?
(Has he/she returned from his/her trip already?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Has he/she returned from his/her trip already?
facts.explanation: vuelto — irregular past participle of volver (to return). Pattern: vowel change in root: vuelto.

facts.quizQuestion:
He {___} tres correos esta mañana.
(I have written three emails this morning.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I have written three emails this morning.
facts.explanation: escrito — irregular past participle of escribir (to write). The -to ending appears in: escrito, puesto, visto, vuelto.

facts.quizQuestion:
Estoy {___} con mi jefe ahora.
(I am talking with my boss right now.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I am talking with my boss right now.
facts.explanation: hablando — gerund of hablar. Regular -ar gerund: habl- + -ando. Used with estar to form the present progressive.

facts.quizQuestion:
Estoy {___} en este momento, ¿puedo llamarte luego?
(I am eating right now, can I call you later?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I am eating right now, can I call you later?
facts.explanation: comiendo — gerund of comer. Regular -er gerund: com- + -iendo.

facts.quizQuestion:
El bebé está {___}.
(The baby is sleeping.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The baby is sleeping.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Present progressive: dormir → durmiendo (o→u stem change)
facts.explanation: durmiendo — gerund of dormir. Stem-change gerund: o→u in the stem: durm- + -iendo. Occurs in -ir verbs with o→ue stem change.

facts.quizQuestion:
Están {___} demasiado dinero.
(They are asking for too much money.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They are asking for too much money.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Present progressive: pedir → pidiendo (e→i stem change)
facts.explanation: pidiendo — gerund of pedir. Stem-change gerund: e→i: pid- + -iendo. Applies to -ir verbs with e→i stem change.

facts.quizQuestion:
Está {___} en la cama.
(He is reading in bed.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He is reading in bed.
facts.explanation: leyendo — gerund of leer. When the stem ends in a vowel, the -iendo ending changes to -yendo: le- + yendo.

facts.quizQuestion:
El libro está aquí. ¿{___} ves?
(The book is here. Do you see it?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The book is here. Do you see it?
facts.explanation: lo — direct object pronoun for masculine singular nouns. Replaces 'el libro' (the book). Position: before the conjugated verb.

facts.quizQuestion:
¿Conoces a María? Sí, {___} conozco.
(Do you know María? Yes, I know her.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Do you know María? Yes, I know her.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Direct object pronoun: la (fem. sing. / person)
facts.explanation: la — direct object pronoun for feminine singular. Replaces 'a María'. Note the personal 'a' before a person as direct object.

facts.quizQuestion:
Tengo los boletos. {___} compré ayer.
(I have the tickets. I bought them yesterday.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I have the tickets. I bought them yesterday.
facts.explanation: los — direct object pronoun for masculine plural nouns. Replaces 'los boletos' (the tickets).

facts.quizQuestion:
¿Viste a mis hermanas? Sí, {___} vi ayer.
(Did you see my sisters? Yes, I saw them yesterday.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Did you see my sisters? Yes, I saw them yesterday.
facts.explanation: las — direct object pronoun for feminine plural. Replaces 'a mis hermanas'.

facts.quizQuestion:
¿{___} llamas mañana, por favor?
(Will you call me tomorrow, please?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Will you call me tomorrow, please?
facts.explanation: me — first person singular direct object pronoun. 'Llamas a mí' → 'me llamas'. Position: before the conjugated verb.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} llamo ahora mismo.
(I'll call you right now.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'll call you right now.
facts.explanation: te — second person singular direct object pronoun. 'Llamo a ti' → 'te llamo'.

facts.quizQuestion:
Quiero {___} antes de comprarlo.
(I want to see it before buying it.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I want to see it before buying it.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Direct object pronoun attached to infinitive: ver + lo → verlo
facts.explanation: verlo — when a pronoun follows an infinitive, it attaches to the end: ver + lo = verlo. Alternative: also valid before the conjugated verb (lo quiero ver).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} di el libro a María.
(I gave the book to María.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I gave the book to María.
facts.explanation: le — indirect object pronoun for él/ella/usted. 'A María' = indirect object → 'le'. Note: IOP comes before DOP. Le + lo/la → se lo/la.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} dije la verdad a mis padres.
(I told my parents the truth.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I told my parents the truth.
facts.explanation: les — indirect object pronoun for ellos/ellas/ustedes. 'A mis padres' = indirect object → 'les'.

facts.quizQuestion:
Mis padres {___} dieron un regalo de cumpleaños.
(My parents gave me a birthday present.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: My parents gave me a birthday present.
facts.explanation: me — first person IOP. 'Dieron un regalo a mí' → 'me dieron un regalo'. IOPs always precede the verb.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} mandé un mensaje esta mañana.
(I sent you a message this morning.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I sent you a message this morning.
facts.explanation: te — second person IOP. 'Mandé un mensaje a ti' → 'te mandé un mensaje'.

facts.quizQuestion:
Quería decirle la verdad. {___} dije ayer.
(I wanted to tell him the truth. I told it to him yesterday.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I wanted to tell him the truth. I told it to him yesterday.
facts.grammarPointLabel: IOP + DOP: le + lo → se lo
facts.explanation: se lo — when both le/les (IOP) and lo/la/los/las (DOP) appear together, le/les change to 'se'. 'Le lo dije' is incorrect; 'se lo dije' is correct.

facts.quizQuestion:
Normalmente {___} a las siete de la mañana.
(I usually get up at seven in the morning.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I usually get up at seven in the morning.
facts.explanation: me levanto — reflexive verb levantarse. The reflexive pronoun 'me' (yo) precedes the conjugated verb. Pattern: [reflexive pronoun] + [conjugated verb].

facts.quizQuestion:
¿A qué hora {___} los fines de semana?
(What time do you get up on weekends?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: What time do you get up on weekends?
facts.explanation: te levantas — reflexive verb levantarse in tú form. Reflexive pronoun 'te' (tú) + levantas.

facts.quizQuestion:
Mi hermano siempre {___} tarde.
(My brother always gets up late.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: My brother always gets up late.
facts.explanation: se levanta — reflexive verb levantarse in él/ella form. Reflexive pronoun 'se' (él/ella/usted) + levanta.

facts.quizQuestion:
Normalmente {___} a las once de la noche.
(I usually go to bed at eleven at night.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I usually go to bed at eleven at night.
facts.explanation: me acuesto — acostarse is also a stem-changing verb (o→ue): acu-esto. Reflexive pronoun 'me' + acuesto.

facts.quizQuestion:
Mi hijo {___} a las ocho.
(My son goes to bed at eight.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: My son goes to bed at eight.
facts.explanation: se acuesta — él/ella form. Stem change: acu-esta (o→ue). Reflexive pronoun 'se'.

facts.quizQuestion:
Cada mañana {___} antes del desayuno.
(Every morning I shower before breakfast.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Every morning I shower before breakfast.
facts.explanation: me ducho — reflexive verb ducharse. Regular -ar verb: duch- + -o, with reflexive pronoun 'me'.

facts.quizQuestion:
Siempre {___} rápido por la mañana.
(I always get dressed quickly in the morning.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I always get dressed quickly in the morning.
facts.explanation: me visto — vestirse has an e→i stem change: v-i-sto. Reflexive pronoun 'me' + visto.

facts.quizQuestion:
Mi hermana {___} muy bien para las fiestas.
(My sister dresses very well for parties.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: My sister dresses very well for parties.
facts.explanation: se viste — él/ella form. Stem change: v-i-ste (e→i). Reflexive pronoun 'se'.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} muy temprano todos los días.
(I wake up very early every day.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I wake up very early every day.
facts.explanation: me despierto — despertarse has an e→ie stem change: desp-ie-rto. Reflexive pronoun 'me'.

facts.quizQuestion:
Hoy {___} muy cansado.
(Today I feel very tired.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Today I feel very tired.
facts.explanation: me siento — sentirse has an e→ie stem change: s-ie-nto. Reflexive pronoun 'me'.

facts.quizQuestion:
¿Cómo {___} hoy?
(How do you feel today?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: How do you feel today?
facts.explanation: te sientes — tú form of sentirse. Stem change: s-ie-ntes. Reflexive pronoun 'te'. '¿Cómo te sientes?' is one of the most useful A2 phrases.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} mucho cuando salgo con mis amigos.
(I have a lot of fun when I go out with my friends.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I have a lot of fun when I go out with my friends.
facts.explanation: me divierto — divertirse has an e→ie stem change: div-ie-rto. Reflexive pronoun 'me'.

facts.quizQuestion:
Mi hermano es {___} yo.
(My brother is taller than me.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: My brother is taller than me.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Comparative of superiority: más + adj + que
facts.explanation: más alto que — comparative of superiority. Structure: más + [adjective] + que. 'que' introduces the second element of comparison.

facts.quizQuestion:
María es {___} su hermano.
(María is smarter than her brother.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: María is smarter than her brother.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Comparative of superiority: más + adj + que
facts.explanation: más inteligente que — comparative: más + inteligente + que. Most Spanish adjectives use más/menos for comparison (no -er/-est suffix like English).

facts.quizQuestion:
Este hotel es {___} el otro.
(This hotel is less expensive than the other one.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: This hotel is less expensive than the other one.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Comparative of inferiority: menos + adj + que
facts.explanation: menos caro que — comparative of inferiority. Structure: menos + [adjective] + que.

facts.quizQuestion:
Pedro es {___} tú.
(Pedro is as intelligent as you.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Pedro is as intelligent as you.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Comparative of equality: tan + adj + como
facts.explanation: tan inteligente como — comparative of equality. Structure: tan + [adjective] + como. 'tan' precedes the adjective; 'como' introduces the comparison.

facts.quizQuestion:
Este examen no es {___} parece.
(This exam is not as difficult as it seems.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: This exam is not as difficult as it seems.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Comparative of equality: tan + adj + como (negated)
facts.explanation: tan difícil como — equality comparison. The negative form 'no es tan X como Y' is very common in spoken Spanish.

facts.quizQuestion:
Mi hermano es {___} que yo.
(My brother is older than me.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: My brother is older than me.
facts.explanation: mayor — irregular comparative of grande/viejo when referring to age or size. 'más viejo' also acceptable but 'mayor' is preferred. Contrast: mayor (older) vs el mayor (the oldest).

facts.quizQuestion:
Ana es {___} que su hermano.
(Ana is younger than her brother.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Ana is younger than her brother.
facts.explanation: menor — irregular comparative of pequeño/joven when referring to age. Contrast with mayor (older).

facts.quizQuestion:
Este libro es {___} que ese.
(This book is better than that one.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: This book is better than that one.
facts.explanation: mejor — irregular comparative of bueno. Never say 'más bueno' for quality — use 'mejor'. Exception: 'más buena persona' (better person) is OK for character.

facts.quizQuestion:
El tiempo hoy está {___} que ayer.
(The weather today is worse than yesterday.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The weather today is worse than yesterday.
facts.explanation: peor — irregular comparative of malo. Never say 'más malo' — use 'peor'. Exception: 'más malo' can mean 'meaner/nastier' colloquially.

facts.quizQuestion:
Carlos es {___} de la clase.
(Carlos is the most intelligent in the class.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Carlos is the most intelligent in the class.
facts.explanation: el más inteligente — superlative. Structure: el/la/los/las + más/menos + adjective + de [group]. 'De' introduces the group of comparison.

facts.quizQuestion:
Es {___} restaurante de la ciudad.
(It is the best restaurant in the city.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It is the best restaurant in the city.
facts.explanation: el mejor — irregular superlative of bueno. 'el mejor' = the best (masculine). Superlatives use the article + irregular form: el mejor, la mejor, el mayor, la mayor.

facts.quizQuestion:
Fue {___} día de mi vida.
(It was the worst day of my life.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It was the worst day of my life.
facts.explanation: el peor — irregular superlative of malo. 'el peor' = the worst (masculine). 'el peor día de mi vida' is a very common phrase.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} más despacio, por favor.
(Speak more slowly, please.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Speak more slowly, please.
facts.explanation: habla — tú affirmative imperative of hablar. Regular -ar: same as él/ella present tense. The tú imperative is used for direct commands to one person (informal).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} las verduras — son buenas para la salud.
(Eat your vegetables — they're good for your health.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Eat your vegetables — they're good for your health.
facts.explanation: come — tú affirmative imperative of comer. Regular -er: same as él/ella present tense.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} tu nombre y apellido aquí.
(Write your first and last name here.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Write your first and last name here.
facts.explanation: escribe — tú affirmative imperative of escribir. Regular -ir: same as él/ella present tense.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} aquí, por favor.
(Come here, please.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Come here, please.
facts.explanation: ven — irregular tú imperative of venir. The 8 irregular tú imperatives are: ven, ten, pon, sal, di, haz, ve, sé. All are monosyllabic.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} cuidado con el perro.
(Be careful with the dog.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Be careful with the dog.
facts.explanation: ten — irregular tú imperative of tener. 'Ten cuidado' (be careful) is one of the most common imperative phrases in Spanish.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} el libro en la mesa, por favor.
(Put the book on the table, please.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Put the book on the table, please.
facts.explanation: pon — irregular tú imperative of poner.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} de aquí ahora mismo.
(Get out of here right now.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Get out of here right now.
facts.explanation: sal — irregular tú imperative of salir.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} la verdad — ¿qué pasó realmente?
(Tell the truth — what really happened?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Tell the truth — what really happened?
facts.explanation: di — irregular tú imperative of decir. One of the 8 irregular tú imperatives. 'Di la verdad' (tell the truth) is very common.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} los deberes antes de salir.
(Do your homework before going out.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Do your homework before going out.
facts.explanation: haz — irregular tú imperative of hacer. One of the 8 irregular tú imperatives.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} al mercado y compra pan.
(Go to the market and buy some bread.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Go to the market and buy some bread.
facts.explanation: ve — irregular tú imperative of ir. One of the 8 irregular tú imperatives. Note: 've' (from ir) is different from 'ves' (you see, from ver).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} amable con todo el mundo.
(Be kind to everyone.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Be kind to everyone.
facts.explanation: sé — irregular tú imperative of ser. Note the written accent to distinguish from 'se' (reflexive pronoun) and 'sé' (I know, from saber).

facts.quizQuestion:
En este país {___} español.
(In this country Spanish is spoken.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: In this country Spanish is spoken.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Impersonal se: se habla (Spanish is spoken)
facts.explanation: se habla — impersonal se construction. Structure: se + third person singular verb. Expresses passive/impersonal actions without mentioning the agent.

facts.quizQuestion:
En este cartel dice: '{___} piso'.
(This sign says: 'Apartment for sale'.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: This sign says: 'Apartment for sale'.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Impersonal se: se vende (for sale — singular)
facts.explanation: se vende — impersonal se. Singular noun (piso) → third person singular verb (vende). For plural nouns: 'Se venden coches' (Cars for sale).

facts.quizQuestion:
En este restaurante {___} muy bien.
(In this restaurant you eat very well.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: In this restaurant you eat very well.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Impersonal se: se come (one eats / you eat)
facts.explanation: se come — impersonal se with intransitive sense. Expresses a general truth without a specific subject. Common in restaurant and travel contexts.

facts.quizQuestion:
¿{___} muy cansado ayer?
(Were you very tired yesterday?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Were you very tired yesterday?
facts.explanation: estuviste — tú form of estar in the preterite. Irregular: estuv- + -iste.

facts.quizQuestion:
Todos {___} de acuerdo en la reunión.
(Everyone was in agreement at the meeting.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Everyone was in agreement at the meeting.
facts.explanation: estuvieron — ellos form of estar in the preterite. Irregular: estuvier- + -on.

facts.quizQuestion:
Él {___} venir pero no pudo.
(He wanted to come but he couldn't.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He wanted to come but he couldn't.
facts.explanation: quiso — él form of querer in the preterite. Irregular: quis- + -o.

facts.quizQuestion:
Cuando {___} la noticia, se puso triste.
(When he found out the news, he became sad.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: When he found out the news, he became sad.
facts.explanation: supo — él form of saber in the preterite. Irregular: sup- + -o.

facts.quizQuestion:
No {___} nada hasta el día siguiente.
(We didn't find out anything until the next day.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We didn't find out anything until the next day.
facts.explanation: supimos — nosotros form of saber in the preterite. Irregular: supim- + -os.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} llegó María de su viaje.
(The day before yesterday María arrived from her trip.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The day before yesterday María arrived from her trip.
facts.explanation: anteayer — the day before yesterday. Like ayer, it always pairs with the preterite for completed past actions.

facts.quizQuestion:
Antes {___} mucho café, pero ahora no.
(Before, I used to drink a lot of coffee, but not anymore.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Before, I used to drink a lot of coffee, but not anymore.
facts.explanation: bebía — yo form of beber in the imperfect. Regular -er: beb- + -ía.

facts.quizQuestion:
Todos los días {___} al trabajo en bicicleta.
(Every day he used to go to work by bicycle.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Every day he used to go to work by bicycle.
facts.explanation: iba — él/ella form of ir in the imperfect. Same as yo form: context clarifies person.

facts.quizQuestion:
Mis abuelos {___} al mercado cada semana.
(My grandparents used to go to the market every week.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: My grandparents used to go to the market every week.
facts.explanation: iban — ellos form of ir in the imperfect. Irregular: ib- + -an.

facts.quizQuestion:
Antes {___} muchas películas juntos.
(Before, we used to watch many films together.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Before, we used to watch many films together.
facts.explanation: veíamos — nosotros form of ver in the imperfect. Irregular: veíam- + -os.

facts.quizQuestion:
Mi padre siempre {___} las noticias después de cenar.
(My father always used to watch the news after dinner.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: My father always used to watch the news after dinner.
facts.explanation: veía — él/ella form of ver in the imperfect. Same form as yo; context clarifies.

chainThemes.name: Preterite
chainThemes.description: Pretérito indefinido — completed past actions, regular and irregular

chainThemes.name: Imperfect
chainThemes.description: Pretérito imperfecto — ongoing, habitual, or descriptive past

chainThemes.name: Perfect & Progressive
chainThemes.description: Present perfect (he hablado) and present progressive (estoy hablando)

chainThemes.name: Object Pronouns
chainThemes.description: Direct and indirect object pronouns — lo/la, le/les, me/te/nos

chainThemes.name: Reflexive Verbs
chainThemes.description: Reflexive verbs — levantarse, acostarse, vestirse, sentirse, etc.

chainThemes.name: Comparatives & Superlatives
chainThemes.description: más...que, menos...que, tan...como, mayor/mejor/peor, el más...

chainThemes.name: Por, Para & Future
chainThemes.description: por vs. para basics, ir a + infinitivo (near future), muy vs. mucho

chainThemes.name: Imperative & Impersonal
chainThemes.description: Affirmative tú imperative (habla/ven/haz) and impersonal se

answerTypePools.description: Decir, ver, and dar preterite forms
Poder, poner, and venir preterite forms
Time expressions used with the preterite
answerTypePools.homogeneityExemptNote: Spanish time expressions inherently range from single words (ayer, antes) to phrases (la semana pasada, hace dos meses). Cannot be split further without losing pedagogical coverage. All answers are semantically homogeneous as temporal adverbials.

answerTypePools.description: Present tense of haber (auxiliary for perfect tense)
Choosing por or para in context + ir a future

synonymGroups.reason: All facts have the same correct answer: 'por' (por_para_choice)
All facts have the same correct answer: 'para' (por_para_choice)
All facts have the same correct answer: 'muy' (muy_mucho_forms)
All facts have the same correct answer: 'mucho' (muy_mucho_forms)
